page: Amazon Web Services c6i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The processor includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector extensions along with hardware-accelerated cryptography such as AES, VAES, and SHA-NI. Memory configuration consists of 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 61949.65 and a Passmark CPU score of 57907.19. It excels in parallelized workloads, showing strong results in software compilation with Clang and ray tracing. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1691.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average throughput, reaching 166.09 GB/sec for large-block read operations.

The c6i.16xlarge does not include local NVMe storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware, which limits its utility for heavy graphical processing or GPU-bound deep learning, though it achieves strong text generation speeds in CPU-based LLM inference, reaching 300.07 tokens/sec on small models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint, making it a resource-efficient choice for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM allocations. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The architecture supports AVX-512 extensions and cryptographic acceleration. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with high scores in software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and memory bandwidth align with average tiers. This instance delivers a balanced compute-to-memory profile, making it suitable for parallel processing tasks, database operations, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-vCPU compute-optimized platform featuring Intel Xeon processors, tailored for parallel processing and high-throughput multi-core workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 128.0 GB DDR4 RAM with a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz with AVX-512 support.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and ray tracing.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
